text.title: cross-tool-migration Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cross-tool-migration as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent-fabricated field maps for unknown custom fields.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents produce plausible-sounding mappings (legacy_id to external_id) that are wrong; unmapped or pattern-matched fields must be reviewed by a human before any load.

failure-mode.description: Running full API load at maximum rate without backoff.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vendor APIs throttle at sustained high rate (429 responses); agents misinterpret these as transient and burn token budget retrying. Implement exponential backoff in the engine, not in prompts.

failure-mode.description: PII and credentials in migration exports not scrubbed before pushing to target.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Issues frequently contain pasted credentials, customer PII, and internal URLs; bulk migration amplifies one ticket's exposure across the entire corpus. Run a scrubber pass before any load.

failure-mode.description: Feeding the entire issue export into a single LLM prompt for mapping.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Long-context drift causes the model to lose accuracy on issues processed later in the window; page by 50–200 issues and let the engine hold full state, not the model.
